title: Threat-Centric NAC (TC-NAC) — STIX/TAXII Feed and Auto-Quarantine Effectiveness
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Threat-Centric NAC* (TC-NAC) category — codes `83100` (TC-NAC threat detected), `83101` (TC-NAC quarantine action taken), `83102` (TC-NAC vulnerability score updated), `83103` (TC-NAC feed import failed), `83104` (TC-NAC adapter connection lost). Vendor-side feeds (Qualys VM, Tenable Nessus, Rapid7 InsightVM, Cisco AMP for Endpoints) feed ISE via the TC-NAC adapters. ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/threatlist/source` and `/api/v1/threatlist/threat` polled every 30 min for the threat-list catalogue and recent threat detections, written to `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:tcnac`. Fields: `endpoint_mac`, `threat_type`, `vendor`, `score`, `quarantine_state`. CIM mapping: `Alerts.severity`, `Alerts.signature` after a `props.conf` mapping.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` ([Splunkbase 1915](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1915)) for syslog. Custom OpenAPI poller for the TC-NAC catalogue feed.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (83100, 83101, 83102, 83103, 83104))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:tcnac")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode=="83100")) as detections, count(eval(MessageCode=="83101")) as quarantines, count(eval(MessageCode=="83103")) as feed_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="83104")) as adapter_drops by vendor
| eval coverage_ratio = round((quarantines / detections) * 100, 1)
| where feed_failures > 0 OR adapter_drops > 0 OR (detections > 5 AND coverage_ratio < 90)
description: Detects TC-NAC adapter connection failures, threat-list import failures, and gaps in the detection-to-quarantine pipeline (where threats are detected by a vendor feed but ISE failed to apply the corresponding quarantine action).
value: TC-NAC is the link between the vulnerability scanner / EDR fabric and ISE network enforcement. When an adapter fails or the feed import errors, ISE's authorization decisions revert to the non-threat-aware baseline — vulnerable endpoints continue to receive full network access. This UC catches the silent integration break.
implementation: Confirm the *Threat-Centric NAC* category is at INFO. Deploy the OpenAPI poller every 30 min. Schedule the search every 15 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression `vendor` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: TC-NAC adapters healthy / total. (2) Sankey: vendor → threat_type → quarantine_state. (3) Top-N: endpoints with highest threat scores, last 7d. (4) Trend: detection-to-quarantine ratio over 30d.
dataModelAcceleration: Optional. Alerts DM acceleration helps cross-correlation panels.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **Vendor scanner maintenance** — Qualys / Tenable / Rapid7 maintenance windows briefly drop adapter connection. Cross-reference vendor maintenance calendars. (b) **Endpoint not currently on the network** — Threat detected by EDR for an endpoint that's offline. ISE has no session to quarantine. The detection-to-quarantine ratio drops legitimately. Maintain a `tcnac_endpoint_offline.csv` lookup updated from active-session data and exclude offline endpoints. (c) **Newly added vendor feed before first sync** — Initial population delay; suppress for 4h after a new adapter is added.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 11.3 requires vulnerability-management identification and remediation. TC-NAC ties vuln-scanner output to network-access enforcement; pipeline-health monitoring evidences the vuln-to-enforcement link is operational.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.41 alert log + monthly TC-NAC operational report.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(e) covers vulnerability handling and disclosure. TC-NAC operational health evidences the enforcement arm of the vuln-handling process.
RA-5 (Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning) requires the system scan for vulns and remediate. TC-NAC's role bridges scanning and enforcement; pipeline monitoring evidences the bridge is operational.
A.8.8 (Management of technical vulnerabilities) — TC-NAC is the operational link.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Disable a TC-NAC adapter (e.g. stop the Qualys API integration). ISE emits 83104 (adapter connection lost). UC-17.1.41 fires showing that vendor with adapter_drops > 0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Re-enable the adapter and confirm ISE emits a new threat sync without errors. Alert clears.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE TC-NAC license (Premier tier).
• At least one TC-NAC adapter configured (Qualys/Tenable/Rapid7/Cisco AMP).
• `tcnac_adapters.csv` lookup with `vendor`, `is_critical`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Threat-Centric NAC* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller for `/api/v1/threatlist/source` and `/api/v1/threatlist/threat` every 30 min.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 15 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in controlTest. Confirm alert.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — TC-NAC Pipeline Health":
• Per-vendor adapter status.
• Detection-to-quarantine ratio trend.
• Top-N endpoints by threat score.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on adapter drop. Slack on detection-to-quarantine ratio drop.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Adapter drops every hour** — vendor API rate limit. Tune polling interval.
• **Detection-to-quarantine ratio = 0** — Authorization policy not consuming TC-NAC threat score; review the auth policy conditions.
• **Feed-import failures** — vendor TLS cert change. Re-import vendor cert into ISE Trusted Certs.
grandmaExplanation: When a vulnerability scanner finds a sick computer, ISE should quarantine it. We check that the message gets through from the scanner to ISE — and ring the bell if the connection is broken or the quarantine never happens.
